A massive, incredibly explosive constitutional crisis violently escalated in the northern region of the republic. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On July 4, 1776, the incredibly massive, universally defining, and absolutely historic United States Declaration of Independence was formally and aggressively adopted by the Second Continental Congress in Philadelphia. Heavily drafted by Thomas Jefferson, the monumental document fiercely declared the absolute, unalienable rights of "life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness." It aggressively severed all absolute political ties with Great Britain, fundamentally and heavily launching the American Revolutionary War and birthing the most massive democratic superpower in global history.

On July 4, 1826, a massive, incredibly profound, and absolutely stunning historical coincidence occurred in the United States. Exactly fifty years to the massive day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, Thomas Jefferson and John Adams—the absolute two most massive, foundational architects of American democracy and incredibly bitter political rivals who later reconciled—both officially and peacefully passed away. This incredibly massive coincidence heavily shocked the young nation, deeply cementing their absolute, immortal legacy in American historical lore.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 4, 1902, Swami Vivekananda, the incredibly massive, universally revered, and absolute spiritual giant of modern India, peacefully passed away in Belur Math, Bengal, at the incredibly young age of 39. Having aggressively and brilliantly introduced massive, ancient Vedanta philosophy to the Western world at the 1893 Parliament of Religions in Chicago, his incredibly massive, profound legacy heavily revitalized Hinduism. He absolutely and fundamentally inspired a massive generation of young nationalist leaders to aggressively fight for a deeply spiritually and politically independent India.

On July 4, 1897, Alluri Sitarama Raju, the massive, incredibly fierce, and deeply heroic leader of the Rampa Rebellion, was officially born in Andhra Pradesh. He aggressively led massive, highly coordinated armed guerrilla campaigns utilizing indigenous tribal forces against deeply oppressive British forestry laws in the Madras Presidency. His massive, legendary resistance and brilliant utilization of the treacherous Eastern Ghats terrain heavily elevated him to the absolute status of a massive, mythical folk hero in the incredibly brutal struggle for Indian independence.
